data.title: American Diplomat Defies Nation for Love; Fired After Secret Affair With Woman Linked To The Chinese Government Exposed
data.description:
U.S. Diplomat Fired After Discovering Secret Relationship With Chinese National

A U.S. diplomat has been dismissed after allegedly concealing a romantic relationship with a Chinese national who reportedly had close ties to the Chinese Communist Party (CCP) — a revelation that has set off alarm bells over national security and foreign influence within American diplomatic ranks.

🇺🇸🇨🇳SEC RUBIO FIRES U.S. DIPLOMAT CAUGHT HIDING SECRET CCP RELATIONSHIP

Talk about forbidden love.

Sec Rubio just gave Foreign Service Officer Daniel Choi the boot after he admitted, on hidden camera, to secretly dating the daughter of a top Chinese Communist Party official.… https://t.co/wf7U453GYYpic.twitter.com/uxkmlAXOF6

— Mario Nawfal (@MarioNawfal) October 9, 2025

The State Department confirmed the dismissal on Oct. 8, with spokesperson Tommy Pigott stating that the diplomat “admitted concealing a romantic relationship with a Chinese national with known ties to the CCP.”

Though the official’s name was not revealed, footage secretly recorded and posted online by political activist James O’Keefe appears to identify him as U.S. Foreign Service officer Daniel Choi.

In the leaked video, Choi is seen speaking candidly about his relationship, acknowledging that the woman “could have been a spy” and that her father was “straight-up Communist Party.” In another moment, he is heard saying, “I defied my government for love.”

“I Defied My Government for Love”: US State Department Foreign Service Officer Dated Senior CCP Leader’s Daughter, Admits “She Could Have Been A Spy,” Refused to Report Her

“Her dad was either a provincial or a federal minister of education. So he's, like, straight up Communist… pic.twitter.com/7Pv1XcZ2x0

— James O'Keefe (@JamesOKeefeIII) August 6, 2025

The conversations were recorded by an undercover journalist and published on X in August, sparking controversy across diplomatic and intelligence circles.

According to reports from the Associated Press, this marks the first known dismissal of a U.S. diplomat under a 2024 policy that prohibits all American government personnel stationed in China from engaging in romantic or sexual relationships with Chinese citizens.

The measure, introduced during the Biden administration, was aimed at preventing potential espionage and foreign coercion risks.

Pigott reaffirmed Washington’s stance in a statement, declaring that the U.S. would uphold “a zero-tolerance policy for any employee who is caught undermining our country’s national security.”

While the case has raised concerns about the personal vulnerabilities of diplomats stationed abroad, it also underscores the growing strain between Washington and Beijing, where love, loyalty, and geopolitics can dangerously intertwine.

data.title: Tether's new chapter: From offshore hegemon to multi-chain stablecoin infrastructure
data.description:
Author: Tanay Ved Source: Coin Metrics Translation: Shan Ouba, Golden Finance

Key Points:

  • Tether remains the global leader in stablecoins, not only providing access to US dollars for emerging markets but also driving demand for US Treasuries through its reserves.

  • With the implementation of new regulatory policies and the evolving market competition landscape, the stablecoin market is showing differentiated characteristics in terms of compliance and profit distribution, and USDT's dominance is gradually declining.

  • The roles of Ethereum and Tron in USDT trading continue to evolve: Tron maintains its leading position in high-frequency, low-cost payments, while Ethereum's declining transaction fees and increased liquidity are driving its widespread adoption in retail trading and settlement scenarios. Emerging channels present new growth opportunities for USDT. Stablecoin-focused networks like USDT0 and Plasma are helping Tether expand USDT's use cases across networks, encompassing areas like payments. Introduction: Today, Tether's stablecoin, USDT, is the undisputed global leader in the stablecoin market, commanding approximately 60% of the $300 billion stablecoin market. Initially primarily a trading tool, USDT's influence now extends beyond the cryptocurrency market—it serves as a key channel for emerging economies to access US dollars and holds increasing geoeconomic significance for the US domestic market. Furthermore, Tether has become one of the most profitable companies in the crypto industry, generating billions of dollars in quarterly net profits and is currently pursuing a $20 billion fundraising plan that, if completed, could make it one of the world's most valuable private companies. However, with tightening regulations and intensifying market competition, the stablecoin landscape is undergoing profound changes. Whether Tether can maintain its network effects and maintain its long-standing dominance will be crucial in determining its future. Based on our recent research on the stablecoin industry following the enactment of the GENIUS Act, this article will analyze Tether's market position—how it balances its current dominance with increasingly fierce competition. It will also explore the evolving trends in USDT's market share, the differences in its application across different blockchain networks, and how emerging channels will impact USDT's future development in the stablecoin industry. Tether's Market Position and Significance Currently, USDT has a total circulation of $178 billion, making it the largest stablecoin by far, far exceeding its competitors (approximately 2.4 times that of Circle's USDC and 3.6 times that of all other stablecoins combined). Its massive scale and ample liquidity make it a crucial tool for safeguarding savings, maintaining economic stability, and facilitating transactions. USDT plays a particularly crucial role in countries and regions with weak banking infrastructure or where local currency inflation exceeds 5%.

    (Data source: Coin Metrics Network Data Pro)

    In addition to USDT, which is pegged to the US dollar, Tether also offers gold tokenization services through XAUt. As demand for alternative stores of value grows, XAUt's market capitalization has exceeded $1.4 billion. Tether is clearly expanding this dual-track strategy: it is collaborating with the Antalpha platform on a $200 million fundraising plan to establish a digital asset treasury and acquire its own XAUt tokens. Furthermore, Tether has increased its investment in Bitcoin and gold mining, gradually building a diversified store of value. Market Share and Growth Pressure Leveraging its first-mover advantage and ample liquidity on exchanges, USDT has developed a powerful "network effect." In the early days of the stablecoin industry, USDT's market share exceeded 80%. Subsequently, with the rise of USDC and BUSD, its share fell to around 50%. The 2023 Silicon Valley Bank (SVB) bankruptcy triggered market turmoil, leading to capital outflows from other stablecoin issuers and a rapid recovery in USDT's market share. However, since 2024, and particularly with the impending passage of the GENIUS Act in 2025, USDT's market share has shown signs of decline again.

    （Data source: Coin Metrics Network Data Pro）

    Against this backdrop, Circle’s USDC has gradually regained lost ground with the advancement of domestic U.S. regulatory policies; while “other stablecoins” (mainly interest-bearing stablecoins such as Ethena’s USDe and Sky’s USDS, as well as tokenized money market funds) are also continuing to expand their market share. The current stablecoin market is undergoing a transition: While USDT maintains its lead in liquidity and adoption, it faces increasing competitive pressure—both from the entry of traditional payment networks and the impact of emerging interest-bearing stablecoins. Despite intensifying competition, Tether remains the most profitable stablecoin issuer, with net profits reaching $4.9 billion in the second quarter of 2025. This profitability is supported by its $127 billion in US Treasury reserves, making it one of the world's largest holders of US Treasury bonds. However, as an offshore issuer registered in El Salvador, Tether's reserves include non-compliant assets such as precious metals, Bitcoin, and secured loans. To address this issue, Tether plans to launch a USAT, a fully compliant US-registered stablecoin, to strengthen its US growth strategy and consolidate its position in the demand side of US Treasury bonds.

    (Data source: Tether Transparency Report (Audit Report as of June 30))

    USDT Circulation Across Blockchains

    After clarifying Tether's market position as the issuer, it is necessary to gain a deeper understanding of the circulation mechanism of USDT across blockchains—the "channels" that support its transfer and settlement. USDT's circulation model is determined by the functional characteristics of each network, which also affects the transaction types and user group structure on different chains. Currently, USDT issuance is primarily concentrated on the Ethereum and Tron networks, with distinct circulation scenarios forming across these chains. Historically, Tron has been the primary channel for users in emerging markets to access USDT, favored for its low fees and fast settlement. In 2025, Tron's average daily USDT transaction volume exceeded 2.3 million, demonstrating exceptional user engagement and fostering a stable flow of funds for high-frequency, small-value payments. This characteristic is highly aligned with its application in retail trading and cross-border remittances—situations where cost-effectiveness and accessibility are paramount.

    (Data source: Coin Metrics Network Data Pro)

    In contrast, Ethereum has traditionally been dominated by large-value, low-frequency transfers, primarily serving as a settlement and liquidity hub for decentralized finance (DeFi) and institutional transactions. However, this landscape is now shifting.

    With the completion of the Dencun and Pectra upgrades, Ethereum's average transaction fee has dropped below $1, making small-value, high-frequency transfers possible. In 2023, the median USDT transfer amount on Ethereum exceeded $1,000, but by mid-2025 it had fallen to approximately $240. Meanwhile, the median transfer amount on Tron increased. This shift has gradually shifted Ethereum's transaction landscape closer to Tron's traditional strengths. (Data source: Coin Metrics Network Data Pro) This shift in user behavior has also been accompanied by a redistribution of USDT supply across chains. In August 2025, the USDT supply on Ethereum ($96 billion) surpassed that on Tron ($78 billion), fully demonstrating how low fees and high liquidity are driving capital inflows back to Ethereum. (Data source: Coin Metrics Network Data Pro (Ethereum USDT, Tron USDT)) Changes in the USDT payment structure across different chains also confirm this trend. On Tron, with the growth of medium-frequency capital flows, the gap between retail and medium-frequency transfers is gradually narrowing. On Ethereum, since 2024, the volume of small-value (less than $100) and medium-frequency (1,000-10,000) payments has increased significantly, while the size of large-value (100,000-1,000,000) transfers has remained stable. This indicates that as the Ethereum network becomes more accessible, USDT's use is gradually expanding into smaller transactions. Expanding USDT's Dominance through Emerging Channels The evolution of USDT's use on chains like Tron and Ethereum demonstrates that settlement speed, cost, and liquidity are key factors influencing user behavior. Looking ahead, Tether is strategically expanding USDT's reach by developing new distribution channels and settlement layers. For example, USDT0, developed based on the LayerZero cross-chain fungible token (OFT) standard, has officially launched. This product enables seamless cross-chain transfers of USDT by locking USDT on Ethereum and minting an equivalent amount of tokens on the target chain, while maintaining a 1:1 reserve endorsement. (Data source: Coin Metrics ATLAS) On September 25, 2025, after the launch of Plasma, a Layer-1 blockchain focused on stablecoins, the amount of USDT0 locked in Ethereum contracts surged from $2.8 billion to $7.7 billion. Plasma supports zero-fee USDT transfers, stablecoin gas fees, and features a high-throughput design. These advantages quickly attracted $6 billion in USDT0 deposits, which currently stabilize at around $4.2 billion. While Plasma's long-term sustainability depends on its adoption in payment and savings (digital banking-like) scenarios, it has become a new complementary channel for USDT—a role similar to the current model of Tron and Ethereum, each serving specific circulation scenarios. Both USDT0 and Plasma demonstrate that Tether is expanding the application boundaries of USDT by covering a wider network, addressing diverse needs ranging from large-value settlements to daily payments, DeFi transactions, and retail use. As stablecoins evolve into global payment infrastructure, Tether's "next chapter" will unfold against the backdrop of intensified competition and clearer regulation. Maintaining its dominant position hinges on its ability to transform from an "offshore issuer" to a "multi-chain compliant infrastructure provider"—all while maintaining its core strengths of liquidity and distribution networks. The emergence of cross-chain USDT (such as USDT0) and stablecoin-focused networks like Plasma signal a diversification of stablecoin settlement and payment scenarios. Whether Tether ultimately expands its network effect or loses market share to competitors will determine the next phase of the stablecoin industry's development.

data.title: U.S. Coinbase Users Can Access DEX Trading and Millions of Tokens While New York Is Excluded
data.description:
Coinbase Launches In-App Decentralized Trading With Millions of Tokens Available

Coinbase has opened a new chapter in U.S. crypto trading by rolling out decentralized exchange (DEX) functionality directly within its mobile app, allowing users to trade tokens instantly after launch.

The expansion, however, excludes New York residents, who remain outside the initial rollout.

Check your phone - the wait is over.

Explore millions of assets, moments after they launch, right from the Coinbase app.

DEX trading is live for all U.S. users (ex. NY).

Coming soon: more assets, more networks, more countries. pic.twitter.com/XryNvDXkdL

— Coinbase 🛡️ (@coinbase) October 8, 2025

How Does the New DEX Work

The feature, which began limited testing in August, enables users to trade Base-native tokens and newly created onchain assets without waiting for formal listings.

Coinbase said,

“Millions of assets can be explored and traded immediately after launch.”

This highlights the app’s expanded scope from roughly 300 listed assets to potentially millions of tokens available on the Base Layer 2 blockchain.

Early projects supported include Virtuals AI Agents, Reserve Protocol DTFs, SoSo Value Indices, Auki Labs, and Super Champs.

Coinbase plans to continue adding Base tokens in batches, ensuring fresh assets appear for users within moments of creation.

Integration With 1inch and 0x Enables Non-Custodial Trading

To provide direct onchain access, Coinbase has integrated liquidity from DEX aggregators 1inch and 0x, enabling seamless non-custodial swaps.

🚨 Coinbase just unlocked DEX trading inside the app!

Now you can access millions of on-chain assets the moment they launch — powered by DEX aggregators like 0x & 1inch.

⚠️ Huge opportunity for early access to tokens, but remember: new assets = higher risk.#Crypto#Coinbasepic.twitter.com/pSkg1aUcln

— TheDeFiMentor (@TheDeFiMentor) September 12, 2025

Scott Shapiro, head of trading at Coinbase, said,

“Together we’re enabling seamless access to DEXes within the Coinbase app, which will bring millions of our users onchain.”

Users can trade with Coinbase balances or USDC, while network fees are sponsored by Coinbase, removing barriers typically associated with gas tokens.

Why Coinbase Moves Into DeFi Now

The launch comes as Coinbase faces declining trading volumes, reporting $237 billion in Q2 2025, up slightly from $226 billion a year prior.

By integrating DEX access, the platform bridges the convenience of centralized exchanges with the transparency and control of decentralized finance.

Onchain analytics and third-party token verification add security by filtering out potentially fraudulent assets, giving users a safer trading environment uncommon in typical DEX settings.

Perpetual DEXs Hit $1.2 Trillion While Traders Shift From Centralized Platforms

Decentralized perpetual exchanges (perp DEXs) have reached a record $1.226 trillion in trading volume over the past 30 days, according to DeFiLlama, representing a 48% jump from August’s $707.6 billion.

Aster DEX led the surge with $493.6 billion, followed by Hyperliquid at over $280 billion.

Perp DEXs are money printers

This is exactly why everyone is moving in.

⤼ Aster grabbed nearly 20% of the entire market.

⤼ Lighter crossed $165B in volume before even launching its token.

⤼ Hyperliquid holds its dominance with $66B volume daily.

Let`s take it from the top… pic.twitter.com/WxVUm1ItKc

— DOLAK1NG (@DOLAK1NG) October 6, 2025

Newcomers such as SunPerp and Lighter DEX are also gaining traction, reflecting a broader market shift toward self-custody and 24/7 global access.

New York Residents Can Finally Stake Crypto

While DEX access is restricted, Coinbase has won approval from New York regulators to offer staking services in the state, marking a significant breakthrough after a year of legal disputes over whether staking constitutes a security.

Oh hey New York, welcome to the party.

Crypto staking is now live in NY. And still live in 45 other states too. pic.twitter.com/IlD9SXBaWf

— Coinbase 🛡️ (@coinbase) October 8, 2025

New Yorkers can now stake Ether, Solana, and other digital assets directly on the platform.

Coinbase said,

“New York’s approval is another proof point that stifling innovation and depriving residents of financial opportunities is bad policy. We applaud New York, and hope to see this momentum continue across the U.S. Staking as a service, like that offered by Coinbase, is not a security.”

The company estimates residents in states with previous bans, including California, New Jersey, Maryland, and Wisconsin, collectively missed more than $130 million in staking rewards.

What’s Next for Coinbase

Looking ahead, Coinbase plans to expand DEX support to additional networks such as Solana and intends to bring the feature to international markets.

CEO Brian Armstrong has described the broader vision as a crypto “super app,” blending investing, payments, and onchain identity tools to challenge traditional banking structures.

By merging DEX trading with staking and onchain liquidity, Coinbase positions itself as a gateway for users seeking both centralized convenience and the flexibility of decentralized finance.

data.title: BNB Knocks XRP Out Of Third Place With 30% Growth in Just One Week— Market Questions If the Rally Is Too Good to Be True
data.description:
BNB’s 30% Surge in One Week Sparks Market Manipulation Accusations

BNB, the native token of Binance, has set the crypto world ablaze by topping the crypto market hierarchy by overtaking XRP to become the third-largest cryptocurrency after soaring 30% in market capitalization.

But while the numbers are dazzling, but the unbelievable speed of this rise is raising eyebrows, with some even suspecting that the rally might even be manufactured more market-driven.

In the span of just seven days, BNB’s price skyrocketed to $1,326, a 30% spike in just seven days. Similarly, the crypto's market capitalisation soared to $182 billion, eclipsing XRP. By comparison, Bitcoin managed only a 5% rise, and Ethereum saw a modest 8.4% increase.

CZ is back in the game and $BNB loves this. Will this spark the broader crypto rally like Jan–May 2021?$BNB just vaulted past USDT and XRP to the #3 spot by market cap at $$177.97B, with a 24h push to an intraday high above 1300$$ for the first time in history.

Under the hood,… pic.twitter.com/M8K7eLDWWS

— Daniel Nita 🇷🇴 (@realdanielnita) October 9, 2025

For a token that usually mirrors broader market trends, BNB’s sudden momentum is anything but ordinary. This meteoric acceleration comes after months of relative stability, leaving analysts stumbling to explain what triggered such an outsized rally in a short time frame.

Rumors of Market Manipulation Stir Controversy

Several factors appear to have supported the move. BNB adoption is expanding rapidly in regions such as Kazakhstan, where a government fund supporting the token was recently established.

The BNB Chain itself continues to grow, hitting a record 60 million monthly active addresses, according to on-chain data shared by analyst Momin Saqib.

Combined with renewed investor interest in the Binance ecosystem—despite its turbulent regulatory past—the environment seemed ripe for a rebound. Still, the scale and timing of BNB’s surge have drawn intense scrutiny.

As BNB’s price continued to climb, accusations of market manipulation began flooding social media. On Reddit and X, users voiced suspicion that Binance may have engineered the rally by buying back large amounts of BNB to liquidate short positions.

“BNB is starting to look like FTX’s FTT token. I can't wait for proof of manipulation, it will be glorious.”

Analyst DeFiTracer went further, accusing Binance of orchestrating “massive buybacks to trigger short squeezes,” suggesting the price rally may not be entirely organic.

🚨 BREAKING:

BINANCE BUYING MILLIONS OF $BNB TO LIQUIDATE SHORTS

THIS IS PURE MANIPULATION! 👀 pic.twitter.com/ZtXBudXlcd

— ᴛʀᴀᴄᴇʀ (@DeFiTracer) October 6, 2025

So far, Binance and its founder Changpeng Zhao (CZ) have issued no direct response to the allegations. CZ, in his usual terse fashion, simply posted: “Keep building on BNB Chain.” The post—intended as encouragement to developers—did little to quell the community’s growing unease.

Keep building on #BNB Chain! 💪

— CZ 🔶 BNB (@cz_binance) October 7, 2025

Trust, Transparency, and the Shadow of Centralization

These rumors highlight one of crypto’s oldest fault lines: the tension between growth and transparency. While BNB’s fundamentals—its ecosystem growth, active users, and regional adoption—paint a strong picture, its ties to the world’s largest centralized exchange make it vulnerable to credibility challenges.

A token’s price can climb rapidly, but confidence is far more fragile. In a market still recovering from the shadow of FTX’s implosion, even the hint of manipulation can shake investor trust, especially when the token in question is so closely tied to a single entity.

FTX Group says it has filed for bankruptcy and its CEO has resigned, marking a stunning downfall for one of the biggest and most powerful players in the crypto industry. https://t.co/NFDfK9aZ5Dpic.twitter.com/pBnf5gkPj3

— CNN (@CNN) November 13, 2022

If BNB truly cements itself as the third-largest digital asset in the world, the implications could be enormous. On one hand, it would mark a comeback story for Binance—proof that its ecosystem remains strong despite years of regulatory pressure and skepticism.

On the other hand, it would also raise critical questions about centralization and market control. A token backed by the world’s largest exchange holding such a dominant market position could blur the lines between open-market valuation and institutional influence.

🚨 BREAKING 🚨#BNB    has just flipped #XRP to become the 3rd largest crypto by market cap. 💥

But the real question is… can it hold the line, or is this just a short-term surge before $XRP strikes back? ⚔️🌊 pic.twitter.com/4TpXVj5r73

— Official Juan O Savin (@JuanOsavins) October 9, 2025

If BNB’s rally is indeed organic, it signals a maturing faith in exchange-based ecosystems. But if manipulation plays any role, the consequences could reverberate across the entire crypto landscape—fueling more calls for oversight, transparency, and decentralization.

Either way, BNB’s rise serves as a powerful reminder: in crypto, momentum can build overnight—but legitimacy must be earned every day.

data.title: The rational beauty of Futarchy
data.description:
Author: David Christopher Source: bankless Translation: Shan Ouba, Golden Finance

Over the past week, the Solana community has rallied behind MetaDAO, the flagship futarchy platform that showcases what happens when markets determine governance proposals.

While MetaDAO may be new to some, the project has been practicing futarchy—a governance model in which prediction markets assess whether proposals will generate "+EV" (positive expected value) for an organization—for years.

The concept of futarchy predates cryptocurrency, but the recent excitement surrounding prediction markets and the ongoing debate over token issuance design have reignited interest. Coupled with MetaDAO's impressive price performance, all eyes have been on the project. Next, we'll break down what Futarchy is, how MetaDAO implements it, and why it holds the potential to solve long-standing problems with token issuance. What is Futarchy? Futarchy is a governance system first proposed in the early 2000s by economist Robin Hanson (also an advisor to MetaDAO). Its core concept? "Voting on values, betting on convictions." In practice, this means the community collectively decides on its definition of "success"—whether it's a higher token value, more users, or any other key metric—and then uses prediction markets to determine which proposals will actually achieve that goal, rather than simply voting on them. Hanson originally envisioned this model for use in governments and businesses, believing that markets could predict policy outcomes. Consistent with the logic of prediction markets, Futarchy's core premise is that the quality of information generated by introducing economic incentives surpasses that of traditional voting. Since 2014, Vitalik Buterin has advocated for the use of Futarchy in decentralized autonomous organizations (DAOs), arguing that it offers a way to abandon hierarchical systems and harness market wisdom. Traditional DAO voting is known to suffer from low participation, whale dominance, and short-term thinking. Futarchy addresses these issues by "economyizing" decision-making. Do you believe a proposal is viable? Bet on it. If you're right, you profit; if you're wrong, you lose. This applies the logic of capitalism to group decision-making—rewarding insightful judgment while expanding the pool of participants beyond token holders to include anyone willing to risk capital, pooling "superior" insights through market participation. How does MetaDAO implement Futarchy? Within the Solana ecosystem, MetaDAO has transformed Futarchy from theory into reality. Initially launched with a modest treasury fund of $10,000, the protocol later secured investment from Paradigm and continued to grow, now offering Futarchy as a Service. Projects like Drift, Jito, and Sanctum are leveraging its tools for token issuance and governance. The detailed process is as follows: Proposal: Anyone can propose a plan, such as "mint 10% more tokens for marketing." There's no approval process required, and the process is completely open. A small fee is required to prevent spam. Market Launch: The prediction market begins. Traders deposit USDC (a stablecoin) and receive two derivative tokens: "PASS" (a bet that the proposal will increase the token price) and "FAIL" (a bet that the proposal will decrease the token price). By trading these two tokens, the price fluctuates based on the collective market's belief. Betting Phase: The market lasts for several days to weeks. If the price of PASS tokens (calculated on a time-weighted average) is at least 3% higher than that of FAIL tokens, the market believes the proposal has a positive expected value (+EV). Result Determination and Execution: If the PASS token wins, the proposal is automatically executed. Traders who bet correctly can redeem their tokens for a profit, while those who bet incorrectly will have their stakes revalued. If the FAIL token wins, the proposal is not executed. A real-world example: MetaDAO passed a token burn proposal, which, despite being against the team's wishes, ultimately resolved the issue of overvaluation. Its spin-off project, mtnCapital, completed its treasury liquidation through market approval and returned funds to holders when momentum stalled. This model is efficient, transparent, and truly empowers holders. How does Futarchy address the token issuance issue? To say that token issuance has come under scrutiny during this cycle is perhaps an understatement. From failed attempts at low circulating supply and high FDV to the commoditization of the linked curve on the Pump (.) fun platform, projects have repeatedly tried new approaches to achieve a "fair launch," but token launches continue to fail. Founders often hog large allocations, sell to retail investors, or make self-serving decisions; ultimately, tokens become mere "governance tokens" with no real power. MetaDAO offers projects using its platform an issuance platform that grants them built-in rights from day one, potentially addressing these common token issues. Fair launches: Projects launch with a high circulating supply of tokens, meaning they are widely distributed to the public and typically without pre-allocated tokens from the team. The token supply is not fixed—the market determines whether minting additional tokens is beneficial (e.g., to raise new funds). This model allows for more adaptability in fundraising, avoiding the limitations of fixed supply models that restrict flexibility. De facto ownership: Through programmable smart contracts, token holders control intellectual property (IP), treasury funds, and key decisions. They can propose anything from setting team salaries to adjusting strategic direction to fully liquidating the treasury. For example, if a founder becomes inactive, token holders can push for a refund proposal, which will pass as long as the market deems it reasonable. This addresses what Felipe of Theia Research calls the "token holder rights problem"—if the founder stops working, holders can get their funds back. Built-in protections: Unlike the "token governance" of traditional DAOs (such as fake voting or endless Discord discussions), Futarchy establishes true accountability. Traders stake real money, which naturally weeds out low-quality proposals. This accountability is particularly important for new project launches, enabling a "pay-for-performance" model where team rewards are tied to verifiable milestones. For example, a recent MetaDAO proposal provides for founders Proph3t and Nallok to receive 2% of the tokens for every $1 billion they increase the META token's market capitalization, reaching a maximum of 10% upon reaching $5 billion. This aligns with the vision of the internet capital markets—an ongoing push to integrate blockchain-native asset ownership and financing models into next-generation corporate governance. However, the problem is that token holders often have no meaningful say in the projects they invest in. MetaDAO's Futarchy changes this—any market participant, regardless of holding a significant token amount, can help determine the direction of the on-chain organization. Ultimately, founders can focus on project development without being distracted by governance, while holders gain verifiable rights and can recover their value through liquidation when necessary. As Colosseum's Mike Rinko puts it, this model makes tokens "valuable, mintable, and founder-friendly."

Future Outlook

Overall, Futarchy has the potential to transform tokens from speculative assets into functional tools with real uses and protection mechanisms. Traditional tokens fail to deliver on the promise of decentralization, leaving holders with little recourse when projects are mismanaged. Futarchy, on the other hand, embeds market-driven accountability mechanisms directly into the token design.

This gives holders real power: they can influence project outcomes, recover value through liquidation when necessary, and ensure that the token supply remains flexible and responsive to proven growth opportunities. Economic incentives reward positive contributions and penalize poor decisions—an approach that fosters sustainable projects in a way that "token governance" can never achieve.

As more projects adopt this model, we will see whether tokens can evolve into reliable vehicles for shared ownership.

Whether Futarchy will become a mainstream governance model remains to be seen. But for projects committed to creating long-term value and wanting to give holders real influence, it has become the most reliable alternative currently.

data.title: SoftBank-Backed PayPay Acquires 40% of Binance Japan to Bring Seamless Crypto Access to Millions of Users
data.description:
PayPay Takes 40 Percent Stake in Binance Japan to Merge Cashless Payments With Crypto

PayPay, Japan’s leading cashless payments provider backed by SoftBank, has acquired a 40% equity stake in Binance Japan, signalling one of the country’s most significant partnerships between traditional finance and cryptocurrency services.

The deal, announced on 9 October 2025, integrates PayPay balances directly with digital asset transactions, aiming to simplify crypto access for millions of Japanese users while enhancing Binance Japan’s domestic market presence.

Softbank & PayPay invests 40% in Binance Japanhttps://t.co/To6pnzeTfm

— CZ 🔶 BNB (@cz_binance) October 9, 2025

How Will Cashless Payments Work With Crypto in Japan

Through this partnership, Binance Japan users will be able to use their PayPay balances to purchase cryptocurrencies and withdraw proceeds from crypto sales into their PayPay wallets.

With over 70 million users, PayPay’s digital money infrastructure is now directly linked to Binance’s regulated crypto exchange in Japan, providing a seamless bridge between fiat and digital currencies.

🚨BREAKING 🇯🇵
Japanese payments giant @PayPayOfficial has acquired 40% of @_BinanceJapan to bridge crypto payments.

How big is PayPay?
▪️70 million users (56% of population)
▪️1 in 5 cashless payments in Japan use PayPay
▪️¥12.5T ($81.6B) in annual transaction volume pic.twitter.com/8ZNe4rM2J1

— Presto Research (@Presto_Research) October 9, 2025

Takeshi Chino, General Manager of Binance Japan, said,

“This strategic alliance is a significant step toward making Web3 more accessible and delivering secure digital asset services across the country.”

Combining Strengths to Build a Seamless Digital Experience

The collaboration brings together PayPay’s extensive user base and trusted payment network with Binance’s global blockchain expertise.

The companies plan to co-develop innovative services that merge cashless payments with digital assets, such as crypto-linked rewards, instant settlement features, and merchant tools backed by blockchain technology.

Not AI. #BNB🤝🇯🇵 https://t.co/6SwzTKi0x5pic.twitter.com/Hzd4DWiYtl

— CZ 🔶 BNB (@cz_binance) October 9, 2025

Masayoshi Yanase, Corporate Officer of PayPay Corporation, said,

“By investing in Binance Japan, the Japanese subsidiary of Binance, the world’s largest digital asset exchange by trading volume, we will provide Binance users with solutions that combine the convenience and security of PayPay. We will continue contributing to the development of Japan’s financial infrastructure by enabling a wide range of financial services tailored to user needs.”

Will This Accelerate Crypto Adoption in Japan?

Japan’s digital economy is rapidly evolving, with growing demand for integration between traditional finance and digital assets.

The PayPay–Binance Japan partnership aims to respond to this demand by making cryptocurrency transactions easier and more accessible for everyday users.

Analysts suggest the integration could increase liquidity and trading activity within Binance Japan, strengthening its domestic footprint while aligning with Japan’s strict regulatory oversight by the Financial Services Agency.

Integration Steps Already in Motion

Initial steps in the collaboration include enabling crypto purchases within the Binance Japan app using PayPay Money and allowing users to select PayPay Money as a withdrawal option.

The move leverages PayPay’s existing cashless ecosystem, including merchant payments, peer-to-peer transfers, and point investments, to simplify access to digital assets for a broader audience.

Chino added, highlighting the ambition to expand the reach of digital finance in Japan:

"By combining PayPay’s extensive user scale with Binance’s innovative technology, we will be able to make Web3 more accessible to people across the country and deliver secure, seamless digital asset services.”

This alliance positions Binance Japan and PayPay at the forefront of integrating cashless payments with cryptocurrency, potentially reshaping Japan’s digital finance landscape and setting a precedent for future collaborations between fintech and blockchain platforms.

data.title: Kazakhstan Sweeps Out Illegal Exchanges in Bid to Build the Next Crypto Capital
data.description:
Kazakhstan’s Crypto Paradox: Cracking Down on 130 Illegal Platforms While Building a Global Blockchain Hub

Kazakhstan is on a mission to cleanse its markets of illicit operations to position itself as Central Asia’s blockchain powerhouse.

In a year marked by bold regulatory reforms and ambitious innovation, the government has shuttered 130 illegal crypto platforms and seized nearly $17 million in digital assets — all while advancing plans for a national crypto reserve and encouraging major global exchanges to set up shop.

🇰🇿 Kazakhstan Seizes $16.7M from Unlicensed #Crypto Exchanges, Shuts Down 130 Platforms

Kazakhstan shuts 130 illegal #crypto exchanges, seizes $16.7M in assets, and uncovers 81 shadow groups with $43M turnover pic.twitter.com/Fz7FCWEhjK

— CryptOpus (@ImCryptOpus) October 8, 2025

According to Kazakhstan’s Financial Monitoring Agency (AFM), the platforms dismantled in 2025 were allegedly tied to money laundering and unlicensed exchange schemes. The agency confirmed that approximately $16.7 million worth of cryptocurrencies were confiscated as part of a sweeping anti-fraud effort that intensified throughout the year.

An AFM spokesman reiterated that its plan is not to stile innovation, but to eliminate risk adn strenghten the legitimacy of the country's crypto sector.

"The platforms we've taken down were operating outside the bounds of the country's regulatory framework."

Cleaning the Streets for the Crypto Future

While the crackdown sounds harsh, it may be part of a larger strategic move: clearing out the underground players to make room for institutional-grade adoption.

Unlike centralized exchanges such as Bybit or WhiteBIT — both of which are licensed by the Astana Financial Services Authority (AFSA) — the 130 shuttered entities functioned as “crypto exchangers”, informal trading shops that facilitated cash-to-crypto transactions without oversight.

Bybit Kazakhstan and AFSA Pioneer Stablecoin Payments for AIFC Regulatory Fees

Mazurka Zeng, Chief Operating Officer at Bybit said, “Bybit Kazakhstan is honored to stand alongside AFSA in shaping a regulatory-first model for stablecoin adoption for AIFC Regulatory Fees”

This… pic.twitter.com/9V04JXeu2h

— Bybit (@Bybit_Official) September 5, 2025

Such peer-to-peer style operations have long been viewed as a weak link in anti-money laundering (AML) enforcement, creating backdoors for laundering illicit funds under the guise of small-scale exchanges.

By dismantling them, Kazakhstan is signaling to global investors that it wants to build a clean, compliant, and trusted crypto ecosystem — not a grey-market playground.

In fact, the number of illegal platforms taken down this year represents a 260% increase from 2024, when only 36 such operations were closed. The aggressive escalation shows Kazakhstan’s regulators are tightening control, even as the government publicly embraces blockchain and digital assets.

Kazakhstan is building a home for crypto founders 🇰🇿

Many thanks to @formacity and @AIFC_KZ for organizing Solana Economic Zone KZ — accelerating technological and economic progress in Central Asia using Solana. pic.twitter.com/AEG2Ir3V5X

— Solana (@solana) June 17, 2025

Kazakhstan’s reform push doesn’t end at enforcement. The government is also pioneering new AML safeguards, such as mandatory identification for card top-ups exceeding 500,000 tenge (around $925).

According to AFM Deputy Chairman Kairat Bizhanov, senders must now verify their Individual Identification Number (IIN), closing another potential loophole in the movement of illicit funds.

These tightening regulations coincide with some of the country’s most forward-looking crypto initiatives. Kazakhstan was among the first in Central Asia to accept regulatory fees in stablecoins like USDT, launch a spot Bitcoin fund, and propose the creation of a state-backed crypto reserve.

🚀 On Sept 23, Kazakhstan’s first national stablecoin EVO (KZTE) was introduced within the regulatory sandbox of the @nationalbank_kz 🇰🇿
EVO opens a new era of fintech innovation, digital payments, and secure blockchain transactions on @solana ⚡️
What’s next with EVO:
🌍… pic.twitter.com/Arhn8yYWEa

— Superteam Kazakhstan (@SuperteamKZ) October 9, 2025

While it remains unclear whether the $16.7 million seized from illegal operators will flow into this future reserve, the broader vision is clear — Kazakhstan is betting that transparency and regulation can coexist with innovation.

A Balancing Act Between Control and Growth

For many observers, Kazakhstan’s crypto paradox mirrors the broader tension facing nations entering the Web3 era: how to foster innovation without enabling abuse.

By clamping down on illicit exchangers while rolling out policies that welcome global players, Kazakhstan is attempting a delicate balancing act — one that could determine whether it becomes a legitimate crypto hub or another overregulated experiment.

If successful, its model could become a template for emerging economies seeking to embrace blockchain without compromising compliance. If not, it risks driving innovation underground, the very space it’s now trying to clean up.

BREAKING: KAZAKHSTAN PRESIDENT RENEWS PLEDGE TO CREATE NEW ‘CRYPTO CITY’ WHERE EVERYONE ACCEPTS #BITCOIN

GLOBAL MAINSTREAM ADOPTION. IT'S COMING 🔥 pic.twitter.com/FwbpBLVit6

— The Bitcoin Historian (@pete_rizzo_) June 25, 2025

Kazakhstan’s hardline crackdown might appear contradictory to its pro-crypto ambitions, but in reality, it’s a necessary reset. Illicit exchanges and unlicensed operators undermine the credibility of any aspiring digital economy.

By forcing bad actors out of the shadows, Kazakhstan is not killing its crypto dream — it’s building the foundation for a cleaner, more trusted ecosystem that could eventually attract institutional money and international recognition.

The road to becoming a crypto hub doesn’t start with hype — it starts with accountability. And in that regard, Kazakhstan may be doing exactly what every serious player in the digital economy must eventually do: clean house before scaling up.

data.title: The top cryptocurrency investor created a $500 million fortune
data.description:
Deng Tong, Golden Finance

During the holiday, the cryptocurrency world was once again swept by meme crazes.

On October 7, 2025, Binance Alpha launched the meme coin "Binance Life," reaping the rewards of many major investors. According to Lookonchain monitoring, a certain address (0x8844) spent 5 BNB (approximately $5,756) to purchase 11.15 million "Binance Life" meme coins before October 5. By the 7th, the value of these coins exceeded $1.6 million. Another trader turned $3,500 into $7.9 million in just three days, a 2,260-fold return.

As of press time, Binance Life was trading at $0.3206, down 34.11% in the past 24 hours.

1. A $500 million market capitalization derived from a single statement by the “No. 1 Cryptocurrency News Lady”

On October 4th, He Yi, the “No. 1 Cryptocurrency News Lady”, replied to a post on X: “I wish you hold BNB, drive a Binance car, live in a Binance community, and enjoy a Binance life.”

II. The Also Popular "4"

In addition to "Binance Life," the number "4" has also recently attracted attention.

The number "4" originated from a phishing attack on BNB Chain, which only netted the hacker $4,000. CZ wrote, "Interestingly, after the hacker sold all the tokens for a $4,000 profit, the community took over the transaction and bought this meme coin at a higher price to mock the hacker. What a hilarious comeback by the community!" According to blockchain analysis platform Lookonchain, a trader with wallet address "0x872" was one of the earliest buyers of the "4" token. His initial investment of $3,000 in BNB quickly grew to $2 million, achieving a return of approximately 650 times his investment. On October 4th, 4 was listed on Binance Alpha. As of press time, BNB was trading at $0.22052, a 24-hour increase of 13.75%.

3. Has the BNB meme season begun?

Changpeng Zhao referred to the growing trading activity on BNB Chain as "BNB meme szn," stating that this was something he "completely did not expect." Marwan Kawadri, Head of DeFi and Head of EMEA at BNB Chain, stated that one of the main drivers of growing investor interest is recognition of BNB Chain's potential for digital asset trading. "BNB Chain has always performed strongly in the DeFi space, but now it's becoming the core of on-chain trading," with both active addresses and decentralized exchange trading volumes reaching new highs. "With the 'BNB meme szn,' you can see the market is beginning to realize that BNB Chain has become a leading trading ecosystem." New cryptocurrency trends may gain traction faster on blockchains because "communities are built around the culture of trading."

Fourth: From "Crazy Zoo" to "Community Identity"

The recent surge in Binance-related memes may be reshaping meme culture.

The progenitor of memecoins is Dogecoin. In 2013, software engineers Billy Markus and Jackson Palmer combined the then-popular "Doge" Shiba Inu emoji with Bitcoin code to create Dogecoin, initially intended to satirize the hyper-heated crypto market.

Since then, various animal-themed memecoins have emerged—dogs, cats, frogs, squirrels, hamsters, hippos... Memecoins have sparked a "Crazy Zoo" craze. Pepe the Frog emojis, in particular, have taken social media platforms by storm, and meme culture has become synonymous with entertainment and humor.

Current memecoins like "Binance Life" and "4" embody more of the "community identity" element. Unlike previous animal memes, the driving factors behind this meme craze include investors' recognition of the value of Binance, a leading exchange, and their consistent trust in the ability of two influential cryptocurrency influencers, CZ, to create myths of wealth.

V. What's left after the meme craze?

The cryptocurrency market has experienced countless booms and busts, and it's no longer uncommon for a meme coin to achieve a thousand-fold increase and then quickly fall. As these myths of instant wealth fade, we can't help but reflect: after the meme craze, what will remain of our industry?

Memes are often closely associated with major IPs, especially when industry celebrities mention a specific term, the market often floods with memes with the same name. In response to this phenomenon, X user @YazanXBT pointed out: "Just because CZ said a word doesn't necessarily mean you should look for a meme with that word and buy it. I'm very confident that when he said something, he wasn't implying anything." CZ agreed: "Good advice. For a while, I tried to avoid meme-related content in my tweets. It didn't work at all. There were too many memes, and it made my writing seem unnatural. Now I tweet normally, and anything meme-related is a coincidence, not an endorsement." OKX founder Xu Mingxing responded to the "Binance Life" craze in X: "Why is OKX Why aren't the team and I promoting 'OKX Life,' 'OE Life,' and '3'? Because we believe blockchain is a technology-driven financial revolution, designed to enable billions of people around the world to enjoy equal and convenient financial services, not to manipulate prices, create sentiment, or guide speculation. The true future of Web3 relies not on emotion or speculation, but on faith and construction."

Jocy@IOSGVC pointed out: "Many exchanges control liquidity and platform traffic, so creating meme-driven wealth creation myths can better gain a leading position and attract countless people... If top players can find ways to focus on long-term value tracks and do more difficult, correct, and long-term things, years from now, countless industry practitioners will regard them as driving forces and be remembered..."

By "holding BNB..." The Binance Life meme craze, sparked by the slogan "Drive a Binance Car, Live in a Binance Community, Enjoy a Binance Life," appears to be cooling. Amidst repeated stories of rapid price increases and price drops, some achieve their dreams of instant wealth, while others shatter their dreams... As mentioned above, from a market perspective, the recent explosion of "Binance Life" and "4" is a wealth myth forged by both "traffic IP" and "community enthusiasm." He Yi and CZ are natural traffic sources, highly tied to investors' speculative psychology. If investor sentiment weakens, the price of meme coins will also fall. Meme coins remain, at their core, high-risk assets with no real application scenarios, no team endorsement, and no value support. Their price fluctuations are driven more by financial speculation and emotions than by real value growth. The long-term development potential of an industry has never relied on short-term speculation, but rather on practical technological innovation, real and tangible application scenarios, and a continuously improving industry ecosystem. This "Binance meme craze," like all previous meme crazes, may ultimately be short-lived. After the wave of wealth creation fades, crypto practitioners should focus on the origins of technology and think about what they should do to make "blockchain" and "encryption technology" famous in the history of scientific and technological development.

data.title: Binance Wallet's exclusive feature "Meme Rush" is now live
data.description:
Source: Binance Official Website On October 9th, Binance announced the official launch of the exclusive "Meme Rush" feature on Binance Wallet. In partnership with Four.Meme, the MEME token issuance platform, this feature creates a dedicated channel for Binance Wallet (keyless) users to discover and participate in high-potential MEME tokens. This feature not only provides a fair and transparent structured token mechanism covering the three-stage lifecycle of new issuance, finalization, and post-migration, allowing users to conveniently operate via the app or website, but also offers early participation opportunities and a 4x Alpha trading volume bonus. Key Highlights Exclusive privileges for Binance Wallet (keyless) users: Verified users can participate in the initial MEME token issuance process, enjoying a fair, secure, and transparent environment throughout. Structured Token Lifecycle: Tokens will undergo a new issuance phase and a finalization phase using a bonding curve model, followed by migration to a decentralized exchange (DEX). Token performance will be fully publicized on the MEME Rankings; top-performing tokens, provided they meet Binance Alpha's functional requirements, may be listed for trading on the platform. Opportunities for Projects: Participating projects will gain access to an active community, gain transparent exposure on Binance Wallet and the "Post-Migration Token Rankings," and strategically position themselves in an environment that builds credibility and fosters growth. 4x Alpha Volume Bonus: Users will receive a 4x trading volume bonus on all Meme Rush tokens during each Meme Rush phase, and for 30 days after the token (if eligible) is listed on the Binance Alpha platform. The "Binance Wallet Exclusive Meme Rush" feature is available through the "Meme Rush" section of the Binance Wallet app or website, which seamlessly integrates with Four.Meme's existing token issuance mechanism. During the "New Release Phase" and "Finalization Phase," tokens are only available to Binance Wallet (keyless) users. Each token undergoes a structured lifecycle to ensure fair participation, transparency, and an orderly market launch. The specific phases are as follows: 1. New Release Phase During this phase, early Binance Wallet users can purchase tokens. Token supply and price will follow a bonding curve model. Tokens are non-transferable during this phase, and all transactions are backed by a virtual liquidity pool to ensure smooth trade execution.

  • Trading Access: Binance Wallet (Keyless) users only

  • Transfer Access: Non-transferable

  • Liquidity Support: Provided by Virtual Liquidity Pool

2. Finalization Phase

During this phase, the token will continue to utilize the bonding curve mechanism while preparations are being made for migration to the DEX. Binance Wallet (Keyless) users can continue to participate in token trading, but token transfers will remain non-transferable, and the Virtual Liquidity Pool will continue to support smooth trading.

  • Trading Permissions: Binance Wallet (Keyless) users only

  • Transfer Permissions: Non-transferable

  • Liquidity Support: Virtual liquidity pool continuously supports trading

3. Post-Migration Phase

Once a token reaches certain milestones (initial requirement: fully diluted valuation (FDV) reaches $1 million at launch), it will enter the post-migration phase. Binance Wallet may dynamically adjust milestone thresholds based on market conditions and user feedback, and all adjustments will be publicly disclosed to the community. During this phase, liquidity will be migrated to decentralized exchanges (DEXs), and tokens will be fully transferable. Furthermore, the token will be included in the Binance Wallet post-migration token rankings, and its performance will be fully public.

  • Trading permissions: Both Binance Wallet users and non-Binance Wallet users can participate

  • Transfer permissions: Fully transferable

  • Liquidity support: Migrate to DEX after reaching milestones

  • Ranking announcement: Full visibility in "Binance post-migration token ranking"

Note: Tokens with outstanding performance may have the opportunity to be listed on the Binance Alpha platform in the future.

For all tokens issued through Binance Wallet's exclusive Meme Rush, if they are evaluated and listed on the Binance Alpha platform, then from the time the token is created to 30 days after its listing on the Binance Alpha platform, the trading volume generated by users purchasing the token through Binance Wallet (keyless version) will be counted as 4 times the Binance Alpha platform points.

Benefits of Project Owners

By participating in Binance Wallet's exclusive Meme Rush, projects can access a high-quality issuance environment, helping their projects grow, increase exposure, and enhance community interaction. Specific benefits include:

  • Direct access to verified users: Connect with highly active, real early users and MEME token enthusiasts

  • Increased token exposure: Display tokens in Binance Wallet and the "Post-Migration Token Ranking" to maximize community exposure

  • Fair and transparent issuance mechanism: Relying on a multi-stage structured issuance process to ensure clear participation rules and build user trust

  • Strategic positioning advantages: Help projects gain an advantageous position in the leading MEME token ecosystem

Mobile App steps:

  1. Open Binance Wallet and go to the Market section

  2. Find “Meme Rush” and toggle on “Binance Exclusive”

  3. Browse and trade your favorite MEME tokens

  4. To track token performance after migration, go to the Market section and click [MEME Ranking]

data.title: Gemini Crypto Exchange Expands in Australia While Crypto Law Remains Uncertain
data.description:
Gemini Expands in Australia With AUSTRAC Registration and Local Banking Integrations

Gemini is strengthening its presence in Australia with the launch of a locally registered entity, positioning itself to capture growing interest in digital assets across the Asia-Pacific region.

The crypto exchange, which has been accessible in Australia since 2019 under its global entity, can now operate through Gemini Intergalactic Australia Pty Ltd, formally registered with AUSTRAC, the country’s financial intelligence and anti-money laundering regulator.

We’re expanding in Australia 🇦🇺

To lead the charge, we’ve brought on James Logan as Country Head. With AUSTRAC registration and new AUD rails, customers now get faster deposits and a smoother trading experience. pic.twitter.com/YACsk1YLsn

— Gemini (@Gemini) October 8, 2025

Why AUSTRAC Registration Changes the Game

Before registering locally, Australian users accessed Gemini’s platform with limited trading options and costly cross-border payments.

Saad Ahmed, Gemini’s head of Asia Pacific, said the new registration allows the exchange to integrate local payment rails such as NPP for instant transfers and PayID for direct account payments, removing the need for transfers to U.S. accounts.

@Gemini launches in Australia with Gemini Intergalactic Australia Pty Ltd! Local AUD deposits via NPP/PayID & AUSTRAC compliance to serve rising 31% adoption. 🇦🇺🚀 #Crypto#Australia#CryptoAdoptionpic.twitter.com/4UjpedAR2G

— CryptoniteUae (@CryptoniteUae) October 9, 2025

According to Ahmed,

“We’re changing that because we think Australia is a market that we want to be in, and it’s a market that we want to grow, so we’re going to build a local team here.”

The exchange will be based in Sydney under the leadership of James Logan, former head of Luno Australia and a senior figure at Bitget, who will oversee strategy, partnerships, and customer growth.

Logan said,

“Australians are increasingly turning to trusted platforms to manage their digital assets, and I’m thrilled to lead Gemini's efforts here and to build a strong presence that will support long-term adoption and innovation.”

What Products Will Be Offered and Regulatory Plans

Gemini is applying for an Australian Financial Services License (AFSL) to expand offerings, including crypto staking, derivatives for wholesale clients, and potentially credit card products.

Ahmed noted,

“We’re not going to have all of the products that we offer in the US, for example, the credit card and staking, out of the gate, but we’re working on it.”

The exchange is taking a cautious approach to Australia’s evolving crypto regulations.

Draft legislation released by the Albanese government last month proposes broader oversight, requiring crypto firms to hold AFSLs under the Corporations Act.

AUSTRALIA TAKES A KEY STEP IN CRYPTO REGULATION. ⚖️🇦🇺🔎

The government has proposed new laws that would apply traditional financial rules (AFSL) directly to the crypto sector.

The Australian Financial Services License is the regulatory framework that oversees financial services… pic.twitter.com/Lkz3HqPVNb

— CHAINISLE (@CHAINISLE_EN) September 29, 2025

Ahmed said,

“There’s going to be some back and forth between the industry and the government for some time. We’ll probably take the position of just observing and seeing how things play out over the next few months.”

He added,

“There is an existing ecosystem here, we’re somebody new that’s coming in, and we just want to make sure that we’re building and focused on our customers.”

Australia’s Crypto Market Remains Attractive Despite Regulatory Uncertainty

Australia has emerged as a major market for digital assets, with Gemini’s Global State of Crypto 2025 report showing 22% of the population owning cryptocurrencies, a figure comparable to the U.S. market.

Source: Gemini

This high adoption rate, combined with a clear regulatory framework, offers potential for both retail and institutional growth.

Gemini’s expansion arrives amid rising competition from global and local exchanges such as Coinbase, Kraken, Swyftx, and CoinSpot.

Local banking integrations offer Gemini a distinct operational advantage in a market where banks have often restricted crypto transactions.

The company’s ability to facilitate seamless Australian dollar deposits and withdrawals may provide a critical edge over competitors.

Can Gemini Turn Global Strength Into Local Success?

While Gemini’s move into Australia demonstrates ambition, Coinlive sees potential challenges in a highly competitive market with complex regulations.

Its strong U.S. presence gives it credibility, but turning that into a local advantage will require more than brand recognition.

The exchange’s cautious regulatory stance may safeguard it in the short term, yet it must move swiftly to capture market share, expand payment and product offerings, and prove it can sustain growth in a region facing mounting compliance pressures.
